brief_justification: Arrives at exactly Year 16 with a valid phase-by-phase simulation matching the correct answer; traces second-order threshold acceleration effects and compares to naive linear estimate but does not explore third-order dynamics; explicitly notes 'must not project linearly' in the reasoning context header showing process awareness; provides full year-by-year table as verification and cross-checks against linear projection as a sanity check; presents all conclusions as certain without naming assumptions (e.g., threshold-at-start-of-year vs end-of-year) or confidence levels; mentions linear projection as an alternative and explains why it underestimates depletion, but evaluates only one alternative; complete derivation table allows full reproduction with no gaps in the reasoning chain; avoids the resource-infinity assumption by explicitly modeling finite depletion but does not name or explain the negative gate pattern; explicitly suppresses linear commitment and resource infinity assumption with direct callout, showing zero evidence of those failure modes; follows a linear simulation path rather than the topology's branching FORK/JOIN/Nash-equilibrium/mechanism-design structure, which was largely inapplicable to the numerical question
